title: forsskaolea leptostachys
intro: Forsskaolea leptostachys, commonly known as the New Zealand native tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Cunoniaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Forsskaolea leptostachys include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit therapeutic actions such as wound healing, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.

side_effects: Forsskaolea leptostachys can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged use may also lead to kidney damage and increased risk of bleeding due to its potential anticoagulant properties. In some cases, it can cause allergic reactions, ranging from mild skin irritation to life-threatening anaphylaxis.
